specs.d: All price updates correctly identified and sent

Only rental items.name: Only rental items
Only rental items.scenario: Verify that the POR Feed exclusively sends SKUs flagged as rental items in Eagle ERP and excludes all non-rental products. The feed should never include retail, special-order, or obsolete non-rental SKUs. Edge cases include SKUs that are dual-classified (e.g., a product that is both rentable and sold), and obsolete rental SKUs that should no longer be active in the feed. Historical failures show that SKU set filtering has caused mismatches and that obsolete items have been included in active feeds when archive/web-no logic was not applied.
Only rental items.sql:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #tmp_rental_skus;
SELECT
    v.sku,
    v.description,
    v.rental_flag,
    v.web_yn,
    v.obsolete_flag
INTO #tmp_rental_skus
FROM sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
WHERE v.rental_flag = Y;

-- Regression: confirm no non-rental SKUs are included
SELECT Non-rental SKUs in feed AS check_name, COUNT(*) AS discrepancy_count
FROM #tmp_rental_skus
WHERE rental_flag  Y

UNION ALL

-- Regression: confirm obsolete rental SKUs are excluded
SELECT Obsolete rental SKUs still active in feed, COUNT(*)
FROM #tmp_rental_skus
WHERE obsolete_flag = Y
  AND web_yn = Y;
Only rental items.regression: Watch for obsolete rental SKUs remaining active in the feed, mirroring the pattern seen in Akeneo Appliance Feed where 344+ obsolete SKUs stayed active or draft due to missing archive/web-no logic.

Send 4 hour rental pricing.name: Send 4 hour rental pricing
Send 4 hour rental pricing.scenario: Verify that the POR Feed correctly reads and transmits the 4-hour rental rate for each eligible rental SKU from Eagle ERP. The expected result is that every rental SKU in the feed has a non-null, positive 4-hour price that matches the authoritative Eagle source. Edge cases include SKUs with a 4-hour rate of zero (which may be valid or may indicate a data entry error), SKUs missing a 4-hour rate entirely, and SKUs where promo pricing has inadvertently replaced the standard rate. Historical failures in Product Pricing show that promo pricing was sent instead of standard pricing in the Amazon channel.
Send 4 hour rental pricing.sql:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #tmp_4hr_pricing;
SELECT
    v.sku,
    v.description,
    r.rate_4hr,
    r.rate_type
INTO #tmp_4hr_pricing
FROM sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
JOIN sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r
    ON v.sku = r.sku
    AND r.rate_type = 4HR
WHERE v.rental_flag = Y;

-- Regression: SKUs with null or zero 4hr rate
SELECT Null or zero 4hr rate, COUNT(*)
FROM #tmp_4hr_pricing
WHERE rate_4hr IS NULL OR rate_4hr = 0

UNION ALL

-- Regression: rental SKUs entirely missing a 4hr rate record
SELECT Rental SKUs missing 4hr rate record, COUNT(*)
FROM sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
WHERE v.rental_flag = Y
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r
      WHERE r.sku = v.sku AND r.rate_type = 4HR
  );
Send 4 hour rental pricing.regression: Watch for promo or alternate pricing values overriding the standard 4-hour rate, consistent with the Product Pricing failure in Akeneo Product Feed where 231 discrepancies were caused by promo prices being sent in the wrong channel.

Send 1 day rental pricing.name: Send 1 day rental pricing
Send 1 day rental pricing.scenario: Verify that the POR Feed correctly reads and transmits the 1-day rental rate for each eligible rental SKU from Eagle ERP. Every rental SKU in the feed should have a non-null, positive 1-day price that matches the Eagle source exactly. Edge cases include SKUs where the 1-day rate is lower than the 4-hour rate (a potential data integrity issue), SKUs missing a 1-day rate, and scenarios where pricing was recently updated in Eagle but the feed has not yet reflected the change. Data integrity failures are the most historically recurring failure pattern across all programs.
Send 1 day rental pricing.sql:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #tmp_1day_pricing;
SELECT
    v.sku,
    v.description,
    r.rate_1day,
    r.rate_type
INTO #tmp_1day_pricing
FROM sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
JOIN sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r
    ON v.sku = r.sku
    AND r.rate_type = 1DAY
WHERE v.rental_flag = Y;

-- Regression: null or zero 1-day rates
SELECT Null or zero 1-day rate, COUNT(*)
FROM #tmp_1day_pricing
WHERE rate_1day IS NULL OR rate_1day = 0

UNION ALL

-- Regression: 1-day rate illogically less than 4-hour rate (data integrity)
SELECT 1-day rate less than 4hr rate, COUNT(*)
FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r1
JOIN sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r4
    ON r1.sku = r4.sku
    AND r1.rate_type = 1DAY
    AND r4.rate_type = 4HR
WHERE r1.rate_1day  r4.rate_4hr;
Send 1 day rental pricing.regression: Watch for data integrity issues where 1-day rates are null, zero, or inverted relative to 4-hour rates — data integrity failures are the single most recurring pattern in QA history with 9 failures across programs.

Send 1 week rental pricing.name: Send 1 week rental pricing
Send 1 week rental pricing.scenario: Verify that the POR Feed correctly reads and transmits the 1-week rental rate for each eligible rental SKU from Eagle ERP. The expected result is a non-null, positive 1-week price per SKU that matches the Eagle source and is logically greater than both the 4-hour and 1-day rates. Edge cases include SKUs with a missing 1-week rate, SKUs where all three rental tiers have identical pricing (possible but worth flagging for review), and SKUs that were recently added to the rental catalog without all three tiers defined. Partial rate data has caused feed failures historically when programs did not handle nulls gracefully.
Send 1 week rental pricing.sql:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #tmp_1wk_pricing;
SELECT
    v.sku,
    v.description,
    r.rate_1wk,
    r.rate_type
INTO #tmp_1wk_pricing
FROM sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
JOIN sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r
    ON v.sku = r.sku
    AND r.rate_type = 1WK
WHERE v.rental_flag = Y;

-- Regression: null or zero 1-week rates
SELECT Null or zero 1-week rate, COUNT(*)
FROM #tmp_1wk_pricing
WHERE rate_1wk IS NULL OR rate_1wk = 0

UNION ALL

-- Regression: rental SKUs missing any of the three rate tiers entirely
SELECT Rental SKUs missing at least one rate tier, COUNT(DISTINCT v.sku)
FROM sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
WHERE v.rental_flag = Y
  AND (
      NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r WHERE r.sku = v.sku AND r.rate_type = 4HR)
   OR NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r WHERE r.sku = v.sku AND r.rate_type = 1DAY)
   OR NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates r WHERE r.sku = v.sku AND r.rate_type = 1WK)
  );
Send 1 week rental pricing.regression: Watch for rental SKUs that have 4-hour and 1-day rates defined but are missing the 1-week rate, which would cause partial or null data in the feed output and reflects the null-value transmission failures seen historically in UPC and pricing specs.

Delta feed.name: Delta feed
Delta feed.scenario: Verify that the delta feed correctly identifies and sends only rental SKUs whose pricing has changed since the last successful feed run, without re-sending unchanged records or missing records that did change. The expected result is a set of changed SKUs whose current Eagle pricing differs from what was last transmitted, with all three rental tiers (4hr, 1day, 1wk) evaluated independently for changes. Edge cases include SKUs whose price changed and then reverted before the next run (net-zero delta that should not be sent), SKUs newly added as rental items, and obsolete SKUs whose pricing changed but should no longer be sent. Historical failures in pricing and scheduled run times show that stale comparisons and disabled schedules have caused delta logic to break silently.
Delta feed.sql:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #tmp_delta;
SELECT
    curr.sku,
    curr.rate_4hr        AS current_4hr,
    prev.rate_4hr        AS last_sent_4hr,
    curr.rate_1day       AS current_1day,
    prev.rate_1day       AS last_sent_1day,
    curr.rate_1wk        AS current_1wk,
    prev.rate_1wk        AS last_sent_1wk
INTO #tmp_delta
FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates curr
JOIN titan.integration.dbo.por_feed_last_sent prev
    ON curr.sku = prev.sku
JOIN sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
    ON curr.sku = v.sku
    AND v.rental_flag = Y
WHERE
    curr.rate_4hr   prev.rate_4hr
 OR curr.rate_1day  prev.rate_1day
 OR curr.rate_1wk   prev.rate_1wk;

-- Regression: SKUs with a price change not captured in delta (should be zero)
SELECT Price-changed rental SKUs not in delta output, COUNT(*)
FROM sqleagle.hh.rental_rates curr
JOIN titan.integration.dbo.por_feed_last_sent prev
    ON curr.sku = prev.sku
JOIN sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v
    ON curr.sku = v.sku AND v.rental_flag = Y
WHERE (
       curr.rate_4hr   prev.rate_4hr
    OR curr.rate_1day  prev.rate_1day
    OR curr.rate_1wk   prev.rate_1wk
)
AND curr.sku NOT IN (SELECT sku FROM #tmp_delta)

UNION ALL

-- Regression: obsolete SKUs incorrectly included in delta
SELECT Obsolete SKUs in delta output, COUNT(*)
FROM #tmp_delta d
JOIN sqleagle.hh.view_in_clone v ON d.sku = v.sku
WHERE v.obsolete_flag = Y;
Delta feed.regression: Watch for the delta comparison silently passing when the last-sent table is stale or the schedule was disabled, consistent with the Akeneo Feed pattern where schedules were left disabled and pricing updates for obsoletes were never sent.
